title: Warm Smoked Argentinian Lamb Grill
description: A sweet and savory dish featuring the rich flavors of smoked paprika, perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat, about 400°F (200°C).
Season the lamb shoulder with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika, making sure to coat it evenly.
Brush the grill grates with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Sear the lamb shoulder for 2-3 minutes per side, or until it develops a nice crust.
Transfer the lamb to a cooler part of the grill, such as the edges or a warming rack.
Continue cooking the lamb for another 30-40 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Remove the lamb from the grill and let it rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing.
Carve the lamb into thick slices and serve immediately, garnished with a sprig of rosemary and a drizzle of lemon juice.
Serve on a lazy Susan spinner for a fun and interactive dining experience.
